Off Calendar Feb-27-2019 Continued To Mar-26-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Pending Action of the Minor, Mileydi Flores.
Dept. 514. Judge: Eric R. Fleming. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Ron Torem, Esq. appeared with petitioner Gabriel Flores, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Mileydi Flores, who was also present for this morning's proceedings.
Petitioner and the minor were placed under oath and testified.
Court Certified Spanish speaking interpreter Manti Henriquez assisted Mr. Flores at today's hearing.
Order appointing Ms. Flores Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed on today, January 3, 2019.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $400,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $100,000.00.
Medical Bills/Liens - $6,094.28.
Costs - $8,399.88.

The net balance of $285,505.84 shall be deposited into a blocked account with Bank of the West, BNP Paribas, located at 7326 Magnolia Street, Commerce City, CO 90022. No withdrawals shall be made without a written Court Order or until the minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Orders Approving the Compromise of a Pending Action of the Minor, and to Deposit Money Into a Blocked Account, were submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to February 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the Status of the deposit of money into a Blocked Account.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Continued To Dec-18-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition To Approve Compromise
Off Calendar Sep-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Eric R. Fleming, Clerk: A. Mok. Proceedings reported by: Gina M. Kessler, CSR#8136.

Thomas A. Schultz, Esq., appearing for Petitioner: Martina Rodriguez, grandmother and guardian ad litem for Minor: Michelle Vera present in court.
Petitioner sworn and examined. Spanish Interpreter Maria-Teresa Poblete appearing for Petitioner and Claimant.

After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:

Total Settlement: $4,000,000.00
Attorney's Fees: $700,000.00 payable to Law Firm of Panish Shea & Boyle, $700,000 to Grossman Law Offices, APLC
Costs: $126,913.00
A net balance of settlement available for claimant $2,473,087; $2,400,000.00 will be deposited into a single premium annuity to fund the future periodic payments and $73,087 to be deposited into a blocked account at: Bank of America, 240 E. Tulare Street, Dinuba, CA 93618 until Minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Court signed Order Granting approval of Minor and Order to Deposit Money into blocked Account.
Status Hearing Scheduled on 12/27/2018 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt for the deposit of money into blocked account.
No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodged with Dept. 514 prior to Hearing.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-09-2018 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-09-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-13-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Jul-11-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PROFESSIONAL CHARTER SERVICES LLC, BRENDA GUTIERREZ's MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE.

Defendants Professional Charter Services, LLC and Brenda Gutierrez's motion to change venue per CCP 397(c) is denied. Longstanding case law cited in the opposing memorandum establishes that a section 397(c) motion must be filed within a reasonable time after the case is at issue. At this stage of the consolidated cases, when more than twenty months have elapsed since defendants filed an answer to the complaint in the first filed action and less than six weeks to trial, it would be extremely unfair and prejudicial to plaintiffs for these cases to be transferred to another county. Therefore, defendants' failure to file this motion within a reasonable time after answering the complaint in the first filed action is sufficient basis to deny the motion. Even if the motion was timely filed, it would be denied since defendants have not shown that convenience of the witnesses and the ends of justice support the transfer of this case to Tulare County.

Counsel for plaintiff Michelle Vera is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-30-2018 Continued To Aug-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-560777 WITH CASE CGC-16-555750
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ron Torem, Esq. 310-276-7878 of Torem & Associates for Plaintiff Victoriana Cruz through Court Call.
Thomas Schultz, Esq. 310-477-1700 of Panish Shea & Boyle LLP for Plaintiff Michelle Vera through Court Call
Oliver Larrivee, Esq. 559-498-6522 of Petrie Dorfmeier LLP for Defendant.

DEFENDANT PROFESSIONAL CHARTER SERVICES LLC, BRENDA GUTIERREZ's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-560777 WITH CASE CGC-16-555750.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues discovery deadlines and experts. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants Professional Charter Service, LLC and Brenda Gutierrez's motion to consolidate this case with case 560777 is granted. Judicial economy strongly favors consolidation of the two cases. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes. The lead case is case 555750. All further filings shall be in the lead case only. The trial date of February 13, 2018 previously set in this case is continued to August 13, 2018, which now applies to both consolidated cases. All discovery and other deadlines are triggered off the new trial date, except that expert discovery is not reopened as to the claims asserted by plaintiff Michelle Vera. The case management conference of March 7, 2017 previously set in case 560777 is now vacated. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
